# Nudge

> A self-hosted, offline-first PWA for recurring tasks and consumable tracking. Push notifications, household sharing, inventory with FEFO, and a sync queue that never drops a tap.

Nudge is a self-hosted progressive web app that helps households and small teams track recurring jobs — medication schedules, filter replacements, pet care, server backups, anything with an interval. It sends push notifications when something comes due, tracks consumable stock lot by lot (First-Expired-First-Out), supports household sharing, and keeps working offline with a sync queue.

## Key facts

- Self-hosted: runs entirely on your Docker stack — no SaaS, no public registration, no telemetry
- Offline-first: reads come from the Service Worker cache; mutations queue in IndexedDB and sync on reconnect with per-field conflict resolution
- Push notifications: due alert at the interval, follow-ups every 2 hours, daily heads-up at a configurable time
- Inventory: attach consumables to routines, FEFO lot selection, expiry warnings 30 days out
- Sharing: share routines and stock items with household contacts
- Languages: English, Spanish, Galician (auto-detected from browser, overridable per user)
- License: MIT — free to use, modify, and self-host

## Tech stack

- Backend: Python 3.13, Django 5.2, PostgreSQL, Redis, Celery
- Frontend: React 18, Vite
- Infrastructure: Docker Compose, multi-arch images (amd64 + arm64)
